cards.back: The OSI model is a conceptual framework that divides data communication into seven layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, and Application.
The TCP/IP model is a simplified version of the OSI model, consisting of four layers: Network Access, Internet, Transport, and Application.
The arrangement of devices within a network, including wired or wireless connections, and how devices interact with each other.
A set of rules and standards that devices use to communicate with each other, ensuring data is transmitted accurately and efficiently.
The process of sending and receiving data between devices over a network, involving encoding, transmission, and decoding of data.
